provisions.title: Short title.
provisions.content:
1. Short title.
This Act may be cited as the Human Resource Development Council Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment and Functions of the Human Resource Development Council
provisions.title: Continuation and establishment of Council
provisions.content:
3. Continuation and establishment of Council
(1) The Council known as the Tertiary Education Council, established under section 3 of the repealed Act is hereby continued under the new name of the Human Resource Development Council and shall continue as if established under this Act.
(2) The Council shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal, capable of suing and being sued in its own name and, subject to the provisions of this Act, performing such acts as bodies corporate, may by law, do or perform.

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment and Functions of the Human Resource Development Council
provisions.title: Objectives and functions of Council

definitions.definition: the Chief Executive Officer of the Council appointed as such under section 18
the Code of Practice issued by the Council under section 35
the Human Resource Development Council established under section 3(1)
a person or entity that provides or organises a programme of education and training, including the provision of professional development services
a member of the Board appointed under section 5(2)

the life cycle stages from early childhood development through formal education (primary, secondary and tertiary education) to skills training and development and employment all underpinned by the process of life-long learning

a human resource development which focuses on, but is not limited to, the planning of the specific elements of a national human resource development life cycle inclusive of tertiary education, skills training and development and employment
the Tertiary Education Act repealed under section 37

a programme which is offered beyond the level of senior secondary education, including technical and vocational education and training (TVET) and such programmes with a set theoretical foundation, through to advanced research qualifications, which have been accredited by the Botswana Qualifications Authority or by a competent authority in the country from which the programme is offered
a public or private post secondary education and training institution including a university save where the context expresses otherwise

skills training and development that is provided at the work-place for employees, apprentices and interns but is not limited to informal and indigenous skills training and development, on the job training and other vocational and technical training other than those offered by a tertiary education institution.
